claim: Claude's memory feature (#) saves repeated instructions in claude.md, allowing local (project) or global (all sessions) reuse of custom instructions
actionable: Use # to save repeated instruction patterns to Claude.md, keeping project-specific setup in one place for consistency across sessions
source_title: 800+ hours of Learning Claude Code in 8 minutes (2026 tutorial / unknown tricks / newest model)

claim: Custom commands in .claude/commands/ directory store reusable prompt templates for repetitive tasks; supports arguments for flexibility
actionable: Build a personal command library in .claude/commands for tasks you repeat (API endpoints, linting, migrations); organize by subdirectory
source_title: 800+ hours of Learning Claude Code in 8 minutes (2026 tutorial / unknown tricks / newest model)

claim: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers extend Claude's capabilities to external tools; Context7 provides up-to-date library documentation
actionable: Connect Context7 MCP server to get fresh library docs; say 'use Context7' in prompts to reference latest framework docs automatically
source_title: 800+ hours of Learning Claude Code in 8 minutes (2026 tutorial / unknown tricks / newest model)

claim: Subagents execute tasks in parallel with isolated context windows, reducing main context pollution; assign tasks (not roles) for best results
actionable: Use /agents to spawn subagents for specific tasks (code cleanup, docs, research) not roles; invoke with @ symbol to parallelize work
source_title: 800+ hours of Learning Claude Code in 8 minutes (2026 tutorial / unknown tricks / newest model)

claim: Claude plugins bundle entire workflows (MCPs, commands, settings) enabling one-command installation and cloning of expert setups
actionable: Create and publish plugins for your workflow; use plugins from marketplace to instantly replicate others' full setups
source_title: 800+ hours of Learning Claude Code in 8 minutes (2026 tutorial / unknown tricks / newest model)

claim: Always review AI-generated code in new session before production; verify security, performance, error handling rather than blindly trusting
actionable: In new session, ask Claude to review files it recently touched for security/performance issues; don't push untrusted AI code to production
source_title: 800+ hours of Learning Claude Code in 8 minutes (2026 tutorial / unknown tricks / newest model)
